pi_items.source: setup_task.sh explicitly generates participant sub-999 with perseverative 'sun' responses, and verifier.py strictly enforces this exact ID.
setup_task.sh generates 100 trials per participant and mathematically groups them via (t - 1) // 20, creating exactly 20 trials per block.
setup_task.sh generates exactly 100 trials per participant, which yields 5 blocks when grouping into 20 trials per block.
export_result.sh and verifier.py both explicitly expect and read from /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_report.json.
export_result.sh and verifier.py both explicitly check for the output image at /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_learning_curve.png.

pi_items.metadata_value: Simulated structural WPT dataset mirroring OpenNeuro ds000231 classification learning
pi_items.verified_value: Synthetic dataset matching Knowlton et al. (1994) cue probabilities
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generates the data from scratch using Knowlton et al. (1994) probabilities via a Python script. A web search reveals OpenNeuro ds000231 is actually a dataset about sweet taste metabolism, entirely unrelated to classification learning.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated Weather Prediction Task (WPT) dataset populated by setup_task.sh based on Knowlton et al. (1994) cue probabilities. The data must be grouped into 5 blocks of 20 trials. The dataset includes a contaminated participant 'sub-999' who must be excluded. The target output paths are explicitly /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_report.json and /home/ga/pebl/analysis/wpt_learning_curve.png. The metadata claim that the data mirrors OpenNeuro ds000231 is completely fabricated, as ds000231 is an fMRI dataset regarding sweet taste and metabolism.
